.. MHS Arcade documentation master file, created by
   sphinx-quickstart on Thu Mar 12 09:37:12 2026.
   You can adapt this file completely to your liking, but it should at least
   contain the root `toctree` directive.

RetroPie
========================
RetroPie is the operating system that powers the arcade machine and playing games on it
It is ran on a raspberrypi 3b, and is connected to the main monitor along with the usb encoders for both player inputs
RetroPie has a lot of details so you can read about those here
Because retropie runs on a raspberrypi it is very easy to corrupt, for that reason there are backups stored
They are stored using Win32DiscImager
If you want to put new games on. Don't, it is a hell you do not want to enter
It took two weeks to figure out how to get new games, we optimized the process, but only one person knows it
Read more in the adding games section
RetroPie is a linux based system, so all linux commands apply
The RetroPie is connected to a usb hub behind the access panel for the ability to connect a usb keyboard and run commands
The RetroPie is connected to the network through the main router, you can also access it remotely through TailScale
For more details on TailScale connectivity, see the TailScale section
All passwords and access information is in the protected document







**hello world**
